We simplify each term using \(\log_a(5^{b}) = b \log_a 5\) and \(\frac{\log_a 5}{\log_{a+1}5} = \log_a (a+1)\).  
The product becomes  
\[
\prod_{k=4}^{63} \frac{k^2-1}{k^2-4} \cdot \frac{\log_k 5}{\log_{k+1}5}
= \prod_{k=4}^{63} \frac{(k-1)(k+1)}{(k-2)(k+2)} \cdot \log_k (k+1).
\]  
The \(\log_k(k+1)\) telescopes to \(\log_4 64 = 3\), and the rational product telescopes to \(\frac{3 \cdot 65}{1 \cdot 66} = \frac{195}{66} = \frac{65}{22}\).
